content.claim: Hungary exhibits exceptionally high between-school variance: school differences account for 71% of variation in reading performance among students, compared to OECD average of 36%, indicating extreme school stratification.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Correlational evidence does not establish tracking as sole cause of between-school variance
Does not isolate contribution of free school choice versus academic selection
Single outcome measure (reading) may not generalize across all subjects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
